name: C++26 Hazard Pointers and RCU
summary: C++26 is the first ISO standard to ship lock-free safe deferred reclamation as primitive vocabulary: `<hazard_pointer>` (P2530R3, derived from Folly's production implementation in use since 2017) and RCU (P2545). HP behaves as a scalable refcount, RCU as a scalable RW-lock; mechanism details are deferred to chapter 9.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: C++26 is the first ISO standard to ship lock-free safe deferred reclamation as primitive vocabulary: `<hazard_pointer>` (P2530R3, derived from Folly's production implementation in use since 2017) and RCU (P2545). Both are complementary — HP behaves as a scalable refcount, RCU as a scalable RW-lock. Together they fill the "how do I free a node that another thread might still be reading" gap that previously required ad-hoc per-project mechanisms or external libraries (URCU, folly::HazardPointer).

sections.title: Mechanism details deferred
sections.content: Mechanism details, reader/writer cost, and retention bounds are covered under hazard pointers and RCU as concurrency-reclamation techniques; this entry only marks the C++ standardization milestone — that as of C++26, both are part of the standard library rather than third-party plumbing.
